verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is stably surface-presented on activated regulatory T cells and some immune cells via covalent disulfide-linkage to the transmembrane protein GARP (LRRC32), which acts as a chaperone-anchor for the latent TGF-β complex. This is a documented, stable mechanism dependent on cell state (immune cell activation) and tissue context (Tregs, microglia). The protein body (LAP/TGF-β dimer) remains accessible from the outer plasma membrane through its own TM-partner-mediated mechanism during the surface state. While TGFB1 is also secreted into the ECM as a large latent complex with LTBP, that pool is not cell-surface-accessible—it is sequestered in matrix. The GARP-anchored surface pool is the actionable surface route.
